blog.title: ContextGo Product Journal and Operational Notes
blog.description: This journal explains ContextGo's product model, remote-access boundaries, and release-operational decisions so the public story matches the real delivery model.
blog.featuredDescription: Each note should explain the product boundary ContextGo is choosing, how it changes real usage, and why it also matters operationally.

entries.title: Why ContextGo starts from context before agents
entries.summary: ContextGo does not start from a chat box. It starts from a working context layer that connects files, tasks, docs, channels, and runtime state.

entries.title: Release operations should flow through contextgo-releases
entries.summary: Website downloads, GitHub releases, and desktop updates should read from one factual source. Otherwise users cannot tell which version is actually real.

entries.title: Desktop host, mobile client: keep the product model explicit
entries.summary: Remote access only stays coherent when the product is explicit: the desktop remains the real execution host, while browser and mobile remain remote clients.
